habitat: Broadleaf; coniferous; and rhododendron forests at 350–3050 m
story: Discover the stunning White Browed Shrike Babbler (*Pteruthius aeralatus*), a member of the diverse Vireonidae family. Native to Broadleaf; coniferous; and rhododendron forests at 350–3050 m, this bird faces various environmental challenges. Currently classified as Least Concern (LC) on the IUCN Red List, the White Browed Shrike Babbler represents the delicate balance of its native region. Understanding its habitat requirements and distribution is key to effective conservation planning. This species contributes to the richness of avian diversity, and this poster offers an artistic glimpse into its world.
Every poster is crafted to inspire curiosity about our planet's incredible biodiversity.
